text:
Once upon a time, upon a quaint little pond, there swam a remarkable fish named Freddy. Freddy liked nothing better than exploring the depths late at night. One particularly twinkling Orion night, he slipped away from his friends beneath the lily pads.

As he glided, he heard an enticing hum coming from a peculiar floating lantern. Curiosity pulled him in. Inside was a singing frog, her voice sweeter than honeyed bees.

"You look like you've had quite the late adventure," she chirped warmly.

"Luckily," Freddy said, "I've just been exploring!"

"The deepest parts? That sounds quite exhilarating!" The frog exclaimed. "One time I found a moonbeam coral that glowed like sunshine."

Freddy darted around the lantern, marveling at all its wonders. Then he saw it—a tiny star that twinkled with a sparkle of diamonds.

"You know," the frog went on, "up here we have the stars. I sing for them
